echo.echo_models module

class echo.echo_models.CarbonAggregation(*, uid: UUID = None, node_name: str = None, ports: dict = {}, node_rule: int = 0, transformations: dict = {}, named_ports: list = [], inflow: str = None, sum: BaseModel = None)

Bases: Node

class echo.echo_models.CarbonSink(*, units: int = 2, initial_value: dict = 0.0, opt_type: int = 2, uid: UUID = None, port_name: str = None, flows: int = 1, import_constraint: int = 1, import_constraint_value: Optional[Union[dict, float]] = None, export_constraint: int = 0, export_constraint_value: Optional[Union[dict, float]] = None, active_periods: dict = None, slack: bool = False, optional: bool = False, import_con_val: str = None, export_con_val: str = None, import_slack: str = None, import_slack_max: str = None, export_slack: str = None, export_slack_max: str = None, pos: str = None, is_pos: str = None, neg: str = None, active: str = None)

Bases: Port

For sinking carbon emissions into an aggregation node

class echo.echo_models.CarbonSinkNode(*, uid: UUID = None, node_name: str = None, ports: dict = {}, node_rule: int = 3, transformations: dict = {}, named_ports: list = [], inflow: str = None)

Bases: Node

class echo.echo_models.CarbonSource(*, units: int = 2, initial_value: dict = 0.0, opt_type: int = 2, uid: UUID = None, port_name: str = None, flows: int = 2, import_constraint: int = 0, import_constraint_value: Optional[Union[dict, float]] = None, export_constraint: int = 1, export_constraint_value: Optional[Union[dict, float]] = None, active_periods: dict = None, slack: bool = False, optional: bool = False, import_con_val: str = None, export_con_val: str = None, import_slack: str = None, import_slack_max: str = None, export_slack: str = None, export_slack_max: str = None, pos: str = None, is_pos: str = None, neg: str = None, active: str = None)

Bases: Port

For doing carbon emissions from an asset (node)

class echo.echo_models.Chiller(*, uid: UUID = None, node_name: str = None, ports: dict = {}, node_rule: int = 0, transformations: dict = {}, named_ports: list = [], inflow: str = None, input_breakpoints: list, output_values: list, max_input: ConstrainedFloatValue, max_output: ConstrainedFloatValue, temp_array: list = None, coeff_array: list = None, n_breakpoints: int = 4, dummy: str = None)

Bases: Node

A chiller is a node that imports electricity and exports cooling power, where the conversion occurs according to a coefficient of performance (COP), which is unitless (kW of cooling energy per kW electrical). We model this nonlinear relationship as a piecewise linear function between input electrical power and output cooling power.

The COP for a chiller depends on the ambient air temperature, and the system loading.

exception echo.echo_models.ConfigurationError

Bases: Exception

class echo.echo_models.ControlledGen(*, units: int = 1, initial_value: dict = 0.0, opt_type: int = 2, uid: UUID = None, port_name: str = None, flows: int = 0, import_constraint: int = 0, import_constraint_value: Optional[Union[dict, float]] = None, export_constraint: int = 0, export_constraint_value: Optional[Union[dict, float]] = None, active_periods: dict = None, slack: bool = False, optional: bool = False, import_con_val: str = None, export_con_val: str = None, import_slack: str = None, import_slack_max: str = None, export_slack: str = None, export_slack_max: str = None, pos: str = None, is_pos: str = None, neg: str = None, active: str = None, min_utilisation: float = None, max_utilisation: float = None, max_power: ConstrainedFloatValue, min_power: ConstrainedFloatValue)

Bases: ControlledLoadOrGen

verify_port()

Used to verify that a port has been set up appropriately

class echo.echo_models.ControlledLoad(*, units: int = 1, initial_value: dict = 0.0, opt_type: int = 2, uid: UUID = None, port_name: str = None, flows: int = 0, import_constraint: int = 0, import_constraint_value: Optional[Union[dict, float]] = None, export_constraint: int = 0, export_constraint_value: Optional[Union[dict, float]] = None, active_periods: dict = None, slack: bool = False, optional: bool = False, import_con_val: str = None, export_con_val: str = None, import_slack: str = None, import_slack_max: str = None, export_slack: str = None, export_slack_max: str = None, pos: str = None, is_pos: str = None, neg: str = None, active: str = None, min_utilisation: float = None, max_utilisation: float = None, max_power: ConstrainedFloatValue, min_power: ConstrainedFloatValue)

Bases: ControlledLoadOrGen

verify_port()

Used to verify that a port has been set up appropriately

class echo.echo_models.ControlledLoadOrGen(*, units: int = 1, initial_value: dict = 0.0, opt_type: int = 2, uid: UUID = None, port_name: str = None, flows: int = 0, import_constraint: int = 0, import_constraint_value: Optional[Union[dict, float]] = None, export_constraint: int = 0, export_constraint_value: Optional[Union[dict, float]] = None, active_periods: dict = None, slack: bool = False, optional: bool = False, import_con_val: str = None, export_con_val: str = None, import_slack: str = None, import_slack_max: str = None, export_slack: str = None, export_slack_max: str = None, pos: str = None, is_pos: str = None, neg: str = None, active: str = None, min_utilisation: float = None, max_utilisation: float = None, max_power: float = None, min_power: float = None)

Bases: Port

A controlled load or generation has a max/min power, as well as a max/min utilisation. The load/generation must be operated within the min and max utilisation (per time unit).

class echo.echo_models.Demand(*, units: int = 0, initial_value: dict = 0.0, opt_type: int = 1, uid: UUID = None, port_name: str = None, flows: int = 1, import_constraint: int = 1, import_constraint_value: Optional[Union[dict, float]] = None, export_constraint: int = 0, export_constraint_value: Optional[Union[dict, float]] = None, active_periods: dict = None, slack: bool = False, optional: bool = False, import_con_val: str = None, export_con_val: str = None, import_slack: str = None, import_slack_max: str = None, export_slack: str = None, export_slack_max: str = None, pos: str = None, is_pos: str = None, neg: str = None, active: str = None)

Bases: Sink

class echo.echo_models.EV(*, uid: UUID = None, node_name: str = None, ports: dict = {}, node_rule: int = 0, transformations: dict = {}, named_ports: list = [], inflow: str = None, units: int = 1, charge_mode: str = None, available: Union[ArrayType, list], usage: Union[ArrayType, list], cp_name: str = 'cp', tod_charging: Optional[Union[ArrayType, list]] = None, interval_duration: int, max_capacity: float, depth_of_discharge_limit: float = 0, charging_power_limit: float, discharging_power_limit: float, charging_efficiency: float = 1, discharging_efficiency: float = 1, initial_state_of_charge: float, trip_slack: bool = False, soc_conserv: float = None, soc_conserv_cost: float = None, V0G_delta: Optional[Union[ArrayType, list]] = None, V0G_SOC: Optional[Union[ArrayType, list]] = None, V0G_trip_infeasibility: Optional[Union[ArrayType, list]] = None, charge_status: str = None)

Bases: ElectricalNode

V0G_charging(interval_duration, force_conv=False)

Convert V0G vehicle (convenience charging) to a soc profile and a power profile if possible.

class echo.echo_models.Edge(*, uid: UUID = None, edge_name: str = None, opt_type: int = 0, vertices: tuple, tariff: list = None)

Bases: BaseModel

Edges are used to connect nodes. For an edge (x, y) where x and y are nodes, the edge value is equal to the flow from x->y plus the flow from y->x.

class echo.echo_models.ElectricalDemand(*, units: int = 1, initial_value: dict = 0.0, opt_type: int = 1, uid: UUID = None, port_name: str = None, flows: int = 1, import_constraint: int = 1, import_constraint_value: Optional[Union[dict, float]] = None, export_constraint: int = 0, export_constraint_value: Optional[Union[dict, float]] = None, active_periods: dict = None, slack: bool = False, optional: bool = False, import_con_val: str = None, export_con_val: str = None, import_slack: str = None, import_slack_max: str = None, export_slack: str = None, export_slack_max: str = None, pos: str = None, is_pos: str = None, neg: str = None, active: str = None)

Bases: Sink

Fixed electrical demand

class echo.echo_models.ElectricalGeneration(*, units: int = 1, initial_value: dict = 0.0, opt_type: int = 1, uid: UUID = None, port_name: str = None, flows: int = 2, import_constraint: int = 0, import_constraint_value: Optional[Union[dict, float]] = None, export_constraint: int = 1, export_constraint_value: Optional[Union[dict, float]] = None, active_periods: dict = None, slack: bool = False, optional: bool = False, import_con_val: str = None, export_con_val: str = None, import_slack: str = None, import_slack_max: str = None, export_slack: str = None, export_slack_max: str = None, pos: str = None, is_pos: str = None, neg: str = None, active: str = None, curtailable: bool = False, port_name_max: str = None)

Bases: Source

Electrical generation which can be fixed (non-curtailable) or variable (curtailable)

Bases: Node

class echo.echo_models.ElectricalPort(*, units: int = 1, initial_value: dict = 0.0, opt_type: int = 2, uid: UUID = None, port_name: str = None, flows: int = 3, import_constraint: int = 1, import_constraint_value: Optional[Union[dict, float]] = None, export_constraint: int = 1, export_constraint_value: Optional[Union[dict, float]] = None, active_periods: dict = None, slack: bool = False, optional: bool = False, import_con_val: str = None, export_con_val: str = None, import_slack: str = None, import_slack_max: str = None, export_slack: str = None, export_slack_max: str = None, pos: str = None, is_pos: str = None, neg: str = None, active: str = None)

Bases: FlexPort

Flexible electrical port

class echo.echo_models.ElectricalStorage(*, units: int = 1, initial_value: dict = 0.0, opt_type: int = 2, uid: UUID = None, port_name: str = None, flows: int = 3, import_constraint: int = 2, import_constraint_value: Optional[Union[dict, float]] = None, export_constraint: int = 2, export_constraint_value: Optional[Union[dict, float]] = None, active_periods: dict = None, slack: bool = False, optional: bool = False, import_con_val: str = None, export_con_val: str = None, import_slack: str = None, import_slack_max: str = None, export_slack: str = None, export_slack_max: str = None, pos: str = None, is_pos: str = None, neg: str = None, active: str = None, max_capacity: float, depth_of_discharge_limit: float = 0, charging_power_limit: float, discharging_power_limit: float, charging_efficiency: float = 1, discharging_efficiency: float = 1, initial_state_of_charge: float, fixed_storage_capacity: bool = True, var_opex: float = 0.0, regularise: bool = False, enable_trip_slack: bool = False, soc_conserv: float = None, soc_conserv_cost: float = None, available: Optional[Union[ArrayType, list]] = None, soc_value: str = None, optimised_storage_capacity: str = None, trip_slack: Optional[Union[ArrayType, list]] = None)

Bases: Storage

class echo.echo_models.ElectricalTellegenNode(*, uid: UUID = None, node_name: str = None, ports: dict = {}, node_rule: int = 1, transformations: dict = {}, named_ports: list = [], inflow: str = None, units: int = 1)

Bases: ElectricalNode

A node that implements a Kirchoff / Tellegen constraint requiring that electrical power is conserved

class echo.echo_models.FixedElectricalPort(*, units: int = 1, initial_value: dict = 0.0, opt_type: int = 1, uid: UUID = None, port_name: str = None, flows: int = 3, import_constraint: int = 1, import_constraint_value: Optional[Union[dict, float]] = None, export_constraint: int = 1, export_constraint_value: Optional[Union[dict, float]] = None, active_periods: dict = None, slack: bool = False, optional: bool = False, import_con_val: str = None, export_con_val: str = None, import_slack: str = None, import_slack_max: str = None, export_slack: str = None, export_slack_max: str = None, pos: str = None, is_pos: str = None, neg: str = None, active: str = None)

Bases: ElectricalPort

An electrical port with fixed values (parameters).

class echo.echo_models.FlexPort(*, units: int = 0, initial_value: dict = 0.0, opt_type: int = 2, uid: UUID = None, port_name: str = None, flows: int = 3, import_constraint: int = 1, import_constraint_value: Optional[Union[dict, float]] = None, export_constraint: int = 1, export_constraint_value: Optional[Union[dict, float]] = None, active_periods: dict = None, slack: bool = False, optional: bool = False, import_con_val: str = None, export_con_val: str = None, import_slack: str = None, import_slack_max: str = None, export_slack: str = None, export_slack_max: str = None, pos: str = None, is_pos: str = None, neg: str = None, active: str = None)

Bases: Port

Flexible port

class echo.echo_models.GasBoiler(*, uid: UUID = None, node_name: str = None, ports: dict = {}, node_rule: int = 0, transformations: dict = {}, named_ports: list = [], inflow: str = None, gas_to_heat_efficiency: float)

Bases: Node

Gas boiler converts gas to thermal energy

class echo.echo_models.GasDemand

Bases: Sink

class echo.echo_models.GasTellegenNode

Bases: Node

class echo.echo_models.Inverter(*, uid: UUID = None, node_name: str = None, ports: dict = {}, node_rule: int = 3, transformations: dict = {}, named_ports: list = [], inflow: str = None, units: int = 1, max_import: float = None, max_export: float = None, dc_ac_efficiency: ConstrainedFloatValue, ac_dc_efficiency: ConstrainedFloatValue, dc_ports: dict = {}, ac_port_name: str = None)

Bases: ElectricalNode

An inverter is a node with one AC port and at least one DC port. Flows from AC to DC, and DC to AC, are subject to conversion efficiencies.

class echo.echo_models.Node(*, uid: UUID = None, node_name: str = None, ports: dict = {}, node_rule: int = 0, transformations: dict = {}, named_ports: list = [], inflow: str = None)

Bases: BaseModel

Nodes are collections of one or more ports that can include non-trivial relationships between the ports, this allows transformations to be implemented.

class echo.echo_models.OptimisationGraph

Bases: Graph

create_path_objects(sources, sinks, regularise=False)

Creates path objects according to source/sink lists provided.

get_ports_on_edge_from_nodes(node1, node2)

Gets the ports that are on the edge from node1 to node2.

get_sources_and_sinks()

Returns a set that contains all source and sink nodes.

lookup_node_from_port(port)

Returns node that a specified port belongs to, if the port belongs to a node.

verify_paths()

Verifies that our paths meet the assumptions required to correctly do flow tracing.

class echo.echo_models.Path(*, edge_ports: List[tuple] = [], vertices: list, uid: UUID = None, path_name: str = None, regularise: bool = False, flow_value: str = None, contingency_neg: str = None, contingency_pos: str = None, path_tariff: str = None, slack: str = None, units: int = 1)

Bases: BaseModel

A path is a sequence of distinct vertices (nodes).

class echo.echo_models.Sink(*, units: int = 0, initial_value: dict = 0.0, opt_type: int = 1, uid: UUID = None, port_name: str = None, flows: int = 1, import_constraint: int = 0, import_constraint_value: Optional[Union[dict, float]] = None, export_constraint: int = 0, export_constraint_value: Optional[Union[dict, float]] = None, active_periods: dict = None, slack: bool = False, optional: bool = False, import_con_val: str = None, export_con_val: str = None, import_slack: str = None, import_slack_max: str = None, export_slack: str = None, export_slack_max: str = None, pos: str = None, is_pos: str = None, neg: str = None, active: str = None)

Bases: Port

The sink for a commodity.

class echo.echo_models.Source(*, units: int = 0, initial_value: dict = 0.0, opt_type: int = 1, uid: UUID = None, port_name: str = None, flows: int = 2, import_constraint: int = 0, import_constraint_value: Optional[Union[dict, float]] = None, export_constraint: int = 0, export_constraint_value: Optional[Union[dict, float]] = None, active_periods: dict = None, slack: bool = False, optional: bool = False, import_con_val: str = None, export_con_val: str = None, import_slack: str = None, import_slack_max: str = None, export_slack: str = None, export_slack_max: str = None, pos: str = None, is_pos: str = None, neg: str = None, active: str = None)

Bases: Port

A source of a commodity.

class echo.echo_models.Storage(*, units: int = 0, initial_value: dict = 0.0, opt_type: int = 2, uid: UUID = None, port_name: str = None, flows: int = 3, import_constraint: int = 2, import_constraint_value: Optional[Union[dict, float]] = None, export_constraint: int = 2, export_constraint_value: Optional[Union[dict, float]] = None, active_periods: dict = None, slack: bool = False, optional: bool = False, import_con_val: str = None, export_con_val: str = None, import_slack: str = None, import_slack_max: str = None, export_slack: str = None, export_slack_max: str = None, pos: str = None, is_pos: str = None, neg: str = None, active: str = None, max_capacity: float, depth_of_discharge_limit: float = 0, charging_power_limit: float, discharging_power_limit: float, charging_efficiency: float = 1, discharging_efficiency: float = 1, initial_state_of_charge: float, fixed_storage_capacity: bool = True, var_opex: float = 0.0, regularise: bool = False, enable_trip_slack: bool = False, soc_conserv: float = None, soc_conserv_cost: float = None, available: Optional[Union[ArrayType, list]] = None, soc_value: str = None, optimised_storage_capacity: str = None, trip_slack: Optional[Union[ArrayType, list]] = None)

Bases: Port

Storage for a commodity.

class echo.echo_models.TellegenNode(*, uid: UUID = None, node_name: str = None, ports: dict = {}, node_rule: int = 1, transformations: dict = {}, named_ports: list = [], inflow: str = None)

Bases: Node

A node that implements a Tellegen constraint requiring that port values sum to zero.

class echo.echo_models.ThermalLoad

Bases: Sink

Positive thermal load is a heating load, neg is a cooling load (ie heat to be removed/exported)

class echo.echo_models.Transform(*, uid: UUID = None, transform_name: str = None, rhs: list = [], lhs: list = [])

Bases: BaseModel

An object for carrying a generic linear node transformation.
